evidence.snippet: Solid-state batteries for  eMobility, defense, and AI/robotics.

Applications Include
Electric vehicles, aviation/drone, power tools, and marine vehicles.

items.notes: Solid-state batteries (FEST® and Solstice™) explicitly listed for aviation/drone, defense, eMobility, and robotics applications

evidence.snippet:
Applications Include
Electric vehicles, aviation/drone, power tools, and marine vehicles.
Solid-state batteries for  eMobility, defense, and AI/robotics.

drone_supply_chain_role.notes: Factorial makes solid-state battery cells (FEST®, Solstice™) that go into drones, EVs, defense platforms; does not manufacture complete drones or drone subsystem assemblies

items.name: FEST®

evidence.snippet:
### FEST®
![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20632%20731'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
FEST® combines a lithium-metal anode, quasi-solid electrolyte technology, and an high-capacity cathode.
FEST®’s proprietary cell design combines performance and safety advantages of solid-state electrolytes with the manufacturability of conventional lithium-ion batteries.

items.notes: Applications include electric vehicles, aviation/drone, power tools, marine vehicles

items.name: Solstice™

evidence.snippet:
##### Solstice™
![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20632%20731'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
Solstice™ leverages a sulfide-based all-solid-state electrolyte material, and can maintain operating temperature over 90°C.
Solstice™ utilizes a novel dry coating process, reducing operating costs, energy consumption, and environmental impact.

items.notes: Sulfide-based all-solid-state electrolyte; applications include EVs, consumer electronics, energy storage systems

items.name: Gammatron™
items.product_type: AI/ML battery digital twin software platform

evidence.snippet:
### Gammatron™
AI/ Machine Learning Platform
![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20428%20200'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%2032%2032'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
Software
Battery digital twin software to enhance battery development throughput.

items.notes: Battery digital twin software for R&D acceleration; not a hardware product

evidence.snippet: By pairing Karma’s 100% America-based engineering, vehicle design, and production with Factorial’s U.S.-developed solid-state battery platform, the collaboration strengthens the manufacturing ecosystem and domestic innovation for next-generation electric mobility. Together, the companies aim to accelerate U.S.-led electric and hybrid vehicle technology advancements while validating solid-state technology in a production passenger vehicle environment.

ndaa.notes: Site emphasizes US-based manufacturing and development ('100% America-based engineering' with Karma, 'U.S.-developed solid-state battery platform') but makes no explicit NDAA Section 848/889 compliance claim

evidence.snippet: **IRVINE, Calif. and BOSTON, Mass., \[February 5, 2026\]** –Karma Automotive Inc (“Karma Automotive), America’s only full-line ultra-luxury automotive manufacturer, and Factorial Inc. (“Factorial”), a U.S.-based leader in solid-state battery technology, today announced the launch of the first solid-state battery production program in the United States for passenger vehicles.
106 Ah Automotive B-Sample shipped to multiple global OEMs for testing

readiness.notes: First US passenger-vehicle production program just announced (Feb 2026) with Karma Automotive; B-Samples shipped to OEMs in 2024; vehicle launch scheduled 2027 — transitioning from prototype to low-rate production

unresolved_questions: IQT (In-Q-Tel) drone/robotics press release was not fetched — would clarify drone-specific product SKUs and possible NDAA/compliance posture
No explicit NDAA Section 848/889 claim found anywhere in corpus

fetch_requests.reason: This press release specifically covers Factorial's expansion to drones and robotics with IQT (In-Q-Tel), which would confirm drone-specific products/SKUs, confirm the component_supplier role for UXV, and potentially contain NDAA/compliance language given the US government/intelligence community connection

tagline: Solid-state lithium-metal battery developer in low-rate production with FEST® quasi-solid and Solstice™ all-solid platforms targeting EVs, drones, and defense applications. Partners include Mercedes, Stellantis, Hyundai. NDAA and Blue UAS certification status unknown; public evidence on defense contracts or unmanned-systems integration remains thin.
